# Claude Code settings

> Configure Claude Code with global and project-level settings, and environment variables.

Claude Code offers a variety of settings to configure its behavior to meet your needs. You can configure Claude Code by running the `/config` command when using the interactive REPL, which opens a tabbed Settings interface where you can view status information and modify configuration options.

## Configuration scopes

Claude Code uses a **scope system** to determine where configurations apply and who they're shared with. Understanding scopes helps you decide how to configure Claude Code for personal use, team collaboration, or enterprise deployment.

### Available scopes

| Scope       | Location                                                                           | Who it affects                       | Shared with team?      |
| :---------- | :--------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | :----------------------------------- | :--------------------- |
| **Managed** | Server-managed settings, plist / registry, or system-level `managed-settings.json` | All users on the machine             | Yes (deployed by IT)   |
| **User**    | `~/.claude/` directory                                                             | You, across all projects             | No                     |
| **Project** | `.claude/` in repository                                                           | All collaborators on this repository | Yes (committed to git) |
| **Local**   | `.claude/settings.local.json`                                                      | You, in this repository only         | No (gitignored)        |

### When to use each scope

**Managed scope** is for:

* Security policies that must be enforced organization-wide
...

Web search results for query: "CLAUDE_CODE_NEW_INIT environment variable Claude Code 2026"

Links: [{"title":"Environment variables - Claude Code Docs","url":"https://code.claude.com/docs/en/env-vars"},{"title":"Claude Code environment variables: Full guide (2026)","url":"https://www.eesel.ai/blog/claude-code-environment-variables"},{"title":"Claude Code’s /init Command Is Getting a Brain Transplant | by ADITHYA GIRIDHARAN | Mar, 2026 | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@AdithyaGiridharan/claude-codes-init-command-is-getting-a-brain-transplant-e0c389ec0c6b"},{"title":"Claude Code Installation Guide for Windows: Git, PATH, Environment Variables, PowerShell, WSL, and Full Troubleshooting (2026) - DEV Community","url":"https://dev.to/xujfcn/claude-code-installation-guide-for-windows-git-path-environment-variables-powershell-wsl-and-1lag"},{"title":"Claude Code CLI Environment Variables · GitHub","url":"https://gist.github.com/unkn0wncode/f87295d055dd0f0e8082358a0b5cc467"},{"title":"Managing API key environment variables in Claude Code | Claude Help Center","url":"https://support.claude.com/en/articles/12304248-managing-api-key-environment-variables-in-claude-code"},{"title":"[BUG] Claude Code isn't picking up environment variables correctly · Issue #32512 · anthropics/claude-code","url":"https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/32512"},{"title":"Claude Code Environment Variables: A Complete Reference Guide | by Daniel Avila | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@dan.avila7/claude-code-environment-variables-a-complete-reference-guide-41229ef18120"},{"title":"Claude Code Installation Guide for macOS: Git, Environment Variables, PATH, and Every Common Fix (2026) - DEV Community","url":"https://dev.to/xujfcn/claude-code-installation-guide-for-macos-git-environment-variables-path-and-every-common-fix-4l96"},{"title":"Environment Variables | shanraisshan/claude-code-best-practice | DeepWiki","url":"https://deepwiki.com/shanraisshan/claude-code-best-practice/4.4-environment-variables"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the `CLAUDE_CODE_NEW_INIT` environment variable:

The new /init, accessible behind the environment variable flag CLAUDE_CODE_NEW_INIT=1, does not scan the codebase and write a file. It interviews the developer.

A member of the Claude Code team at Anthropic announced a research preview on March 23 that begins to close this gap. The original /init operated bottom-up: inspect code, infer context, produce output. The new version inverts this. Using Claude Code's own AskUserQuestion tool, it opens a structured conversation — asking about workflow patterns, recurring tasks that could be codified into skills, and the kinds of errors or unsafe operations worth blocking through hooks.

The flag to enable it is CLAUDE_CODE_NEW_INIT=1 before the claude invocation.

In essence, this is a new feature released in March 2026 that makes the `/init` command conversational rather than automated. Instead of scanning your codebase to infer configuration, it asks you questions to understand your needs and then generates an appropriate project configuration.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

# Environment variables

> Complete reference for environment variables that control Claude Code behavior.

Claude Code supports the following environment variables to control its behavior. Set them in your shell before launching `claude`, or configure them in [`settings.json`](/en/settings#available-settings) under the `env` key to apply them to every session or roll them out across your team.

| Variable                                       | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| :--------------------------------------------- | :--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
...
